title: Bridge Essay: Pleasure seeking and birdsong.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** (Phase 5) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Water/Pleasure to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Pleasure seeking and birdsong.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev. PMID: 21251924. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2010.12.017. Songbirds sing at high rates within multiple contexts, suggesting that they are highly motivated to communicate and that the act of singing itself may be rewarding. Little is known about the neural regulation of the motivation to communicate. Dopamine and opioid neuropeptides play aprimary role in reward seeking and sensory pleasure. In songbirds, these neurochemicals are found within brain regions implicated in both motivation and reward, including the medial preoptic nucleus (mPOA) and ventra
